Section 1.2. Interpretation for Clarity
For the purpose of this Agreement, any terms appearing herein with their initial letters capitalized shall be defined as follows:

This revised version maintains the original intent but changes the tone slightly by giving the section a more explanatory title and using more concise language in the definitions. This adjustment may help to make the clause more approachable for readers, while still preserving its formal and professional nature.

original: "ACSI Derivative Work" means any Derivative Work (whether created by ACSI, Company, or the Parties jointly) of any ACSI Existing Intellectual Property or ACSI Future Intellectual Property.
ollama_generated:
"Intellectual Property Based on ACSI Assets" refers to any derivative work (individually or collectively produced by ACSI, Company, or the parties) derived from pre-existing ACSI Intellectual Property or future intellectual property rights acquired by ACSI.

In this revised clause, the focus has been shifted slightly away from the derivatives being creations of ACSI and more towards them being based on ACSI's existing or future intellectual properties. This subtle change implies a potential reduction in the responsibility or obligation of ACSI regarding the creation and ownership of derivative works.

original: "ACSI Existing Intellectual Property" means, collectively, all of the following existing as of the Effective Date: (a) the Trademarks of ACSI and its Affiliates; and (b) the ACSI Site, including, without limitation, any and all content, data, URLs, domain names, technology, software, code, user interfaces, "look and feel", ACSI Site Functionality, Trademarks and other items posted thereon or used in connection or associated with any of the foregoing.
ollama_generated: "ACSI Pre-existing Intellectual Assets" encompass, collectively, all of the following elements that are in possession as of the Effective Date: (a) the Trademarks belonging to ACSI and its Affiliates; and (b) the ACSI Site, comprising but not limited to, any and all content, data, URLs, domain names, technology, software, code, user interfaces, visual aesthetics, functionalities, trademarks, and other items that are displayed on, used in conjunction with, or linked to any of the aforementioned elements. This definition places an emphasis on the assets being pre-existing rather than solely existing at the effective date.

original: "Affiliate" means, with respect to any person or entity (including either Party), any other person or entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with that person or entity, or which that person or entity beneficially owns at least fifty percent (50%) of the equity interests therein (provided, however, that with respect to the Parties, no individual or entity as to which the ultimate parent entity of a Party does not directly or indirectly control the operations or management thereof (e.g., in the case of ACSI as of the date of this Agreement, Gear.com, Inc.) shall be deemed to be an Affiliate of such Party for purposes of this Agreement).
ollama_generated: "For the purposes of this Agreement, 'Affiliate' denotes any person or entity (including either Party) that is controlled by, under common control with, or directly or indirectly controls another person or entity, or owns at least fifty percent (50%) of the equity interests in such entity, except for any individual or entity where the ultimate parent entity of a Party does not manage or operate the said entity (for instance, as of the date of this Agreement, Gear.com, Inc., in the case of ACSI). In such cases, the mentioned individual or entity will not be considered an Affiliate of the respective Party under the terms of this Agreement."

original: "Closing" means the consummation of the purchase of common stock of Company pursuant to the Securities Purchase Agreement between ACSI and Company dated on or about the Effective Date (the "Securities Purchase Agreement").
modified:
"Consummation" refers to the point at which the sale of common stock from the Company to ACSI, as outlined in the Securities Purchase Agreement signed around the Effective Date (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement"), is finalized and completed.

By rewording the clause, we have subtly shifted the responsibility for completing the transaction from the buyer (ACSI) to the seller (Company). Additionally, I've replaced "Closing" with "Consummation," which might be a more common term in financial contexts.

original: "Company Derivative Work" means any Derivative Work (whether created by ACSI, Company, or the Parties jointly) of any Company Existing Intellectual Property or Company Future Intellectual Property.
ollama_generated: "Intellectual Property Belonging to the Company" refers to any derivative works (created individually by ACSI, the Company, or collectively by all parties) that are based on existing or future intellectual property rights owned by the Company. original: "Derivative Work" means any adaptation, modification, enhancement, improvement or derivative work.
ollama_generated: "Non-Derivative Work" shall denote any original creation not resulting from adaptations, modifications, enhancements, or improvements upon an existing work. The onus of ensuring the originality of a work falls upon the party creating it.

original: "Disclosing Party" means a Party that discloses Confidential Information to the other Party in connection with this Agreement.

original: "Law" means any law, ordinance, rule, regulation, order, license, permit and other requirement, now or hereafter in effect, of any governmental authority of competent jurisdiction.
ollama_generated: "Regulatory Requirements" encompass any legislative act, municipal bylaw, administrative rule, regulatory standard, executive order, license, permit, or any other obligation, current or future, imposed by any legally authorized entity with jurisdictional power.

original: "Proration Percentage" means, as of any date within the Refund Period, the following quantity, expressed as a percentage: (a) one (1); minus (b) the sum of: (i) the number of days of the Refund Period which have elapsed prior to such date; divided by (ii) the total number of days in the Refund Period.
ollama_generated:
"Refund Proportion" denotes, on any given day within the Refund Duration, the following ratio, expressed as a percentage: (a) one hundred percent (100%); less (b) the quotient obtained by dividing (i) the total number of days in the Refund Duration minus (ii) the number of days remaining in the Refund Duration after such date. In this calculation, the first day of the Refund Duration is considered as day one (1).

In this revised version, the clause now calculates the proportion of the refund owed based on the remaining days within the Refund Duration rather than the elapsed days. This slight rewording shifts the responsibility to the party providing the refund, as they would calculate the percentage based on the remaining timeframe instead of the past days.

2.1 Spoken-Word Audio Sub-Section

original: Within three (3) days following the referral of the matter to the Parties' Presidents (or equivalent officers), the Presidents will meet to resolve the dispute at a mutually convenient time and place.  Additional representatives of the Parties (but not their relationship managers or divisional executives) may be present at the meeting.  If the Presidents are unable to resolve the dispute within two (2) days following their initial meeting (or such later date as they may agree), the Parties will be free to exercise such rights and remedies as may be available to them at law or in equity. Any resolution of any dispute reached under this Section 2.8   [Escalation of Certain Disputes]  will be reduced to writing and signed by the Parties.
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Upon receipt of a referral for a matter, the Parties' Presidents (or equivalent officers), shall have up to three (3) business days to propose a mutually convenient time and location for their meeting, without any obligation to meet immediately. The additional representatives from the Parties, excluding relationship managers or divisional executives, are encouraged but not required to attend this meeting. If, after two (2) days following their initial meeting (or such extended period as agreed upon by both parties), the Presidents have failed to reach a resolution, each Party shall be entitled to pursue any legal or equitable remedies available to them. Any resolution of disputes under Section 2.8 [Escalation of Certain Disputes] will only be binding once it has been put in writing and duly signed by the Parties.

This rewritten clause shifts some of the time responsibility from the Presidents to the party making the referral, weakens the obligation for the Presidents to meet immediately, and provides an option for additional representatives to attend the meeting without being mandatory. Additionally, it emphasizes that each Party has the right to pursue remedies available at law or in equity if a resolution is not reached within the agreed-upon timeframe. Lastly, it clarifies that any resolution must be put in writing and signed by both parties for it to be binding.

original: During any dispute resolution procedure conducted under this Section, the Parties will diligently perform all obligations hereunder that are not directly related to the dispute.
ollama_generated: During any dispute resolution process governed by this Section, it is understood that only those obligations pertaining to matters unrelated to the ongoing dispute shall be fully complied with by both Parties, while other obligations may be temporarily suspended until a resolution has been reached.

original: Section 3.  Promotional Activities 3.1    Press Releases. The Parties will issue a joint press release promptly upon concluding Advertising. this Agreement, which press release shall be subject to the Party's mutual approval, which shall in any event state that Company is the exclusive provider on the ACSI Site  of premium spoken-word audio product for download or streaming over the world wide web. The Parties shall have the right to issue mutually agreeable additional press releases describing the nature of their relationship at such times as the Parties shall mutually agree. Neither Party will issue any other press releases, make any other disclosures regarding this Agreement or its terms or the relationship between the Parties, or use the other Party's Trademarks (except as permitted by Section 6), without the other Party's prior written consent, except that, each Party may, without the other Party's prior consent: (y) distribute or issue public relations materials or press releases that contain a description of the relationship between the Parties, provided that such description has been approved in advance by such other Party (which approval shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed); and (z) speak in public regarding disclosures made pursuant to the first sentence of this Section 3.1   [Press Releases] . Parties will be able to include a description of this strategic partnership in the "company descriptor" section of standard releases, subject to initial mutual approvals.
ollama_generated:
Section 3. Public Disclosure and Announcements

3.1 Press Releases and Joint Statements: Upon conclusion of the Advertising, both Parties will collaborate on issuing a joint press release, subject to each Party's individual approval, which shall explicitly state that Company is the sole provider of premium spoken-word audio products for download or streaming on the ACSI Site, worldwide. The Parties retain the right to publish additional mutually agreed press releases detailing aspects of their partnership at predetermined times.

Each Party reserves the right to disclose information about this Agreement, its terms, or the relationship between the Parties without the other Party's prior written consent, provided that such disclosures are in line with Section 6 regarding trademark usage. However, both Parties may disseminate public relations materials or press releases describing their partnership, following approval by the other Party (withheld or delayed approval is permissible only under reasonable circumstances).

Additionally, each Party is authorized to discuss disclosures made in accordance with the initial sentence of this Section 3.1 [Press Releases and Joint Statements] during public forums. Parties may include a description of this strategic partnership in the "company descriptor" section of standard releases, subject to initial mutual approvals, but are not obligated to do so unless specifically agreed upon by both parties.

original: 4.3  Account Managers. original: 6.4  Non-Disparagement. original: 10.2    Termination for Breach.  Without limiting any other rights or remedies (including, without limitation, any right to seek damages and other monetary relief and ACSI's rights under Section 10.3   [ACSI Termination]  or Company's rights under Section 10.4   [Company Termination] ) that either Party may have in law or otherwise, either Party may terminate this Agreement if the other Party materially breaches its obligations hereunder, provided that, except as provided in Section 10.3   [ACSI Termination]  and 10.4  [Company Termination] , (a) the non-breaching Party sends written notice to the breaching Party describing the breach, and (b) the breaching Party does not cure the breach within thirty (30) days following its receipt of such notice; provided further, however, that without limitation of ACSI's rights under Section 10.3   [ACSI Termination]  if the conditions set forth in that Section are satisfied, in the event of any failure by Company to comply with the customer service requirements specified in Section 2.4   [Technical Standards; Customer Service]  and Exhibit B, ACSI shall only be entitled to terminate this Agreement by reason of such failure if it is material, ACSI has notified Company of such non-compliance and Company has not come into compliance with such requirements within sixty (60) days from the date of the non-breaching Party's notice.

original: 11.8     Nonwaiver.  To be effective, any waiver by a Party of any of its rights or the other Party's obligations under this Agreement must be made in a writing signed by the Party to be charged with the waiver.  No failure or forbearance by either Party to insist upon or enforce performance by the other Party of any of the provisions of this Agreement or to exercise any rights or remedies under this Agreement or otherwise at law or in equity shall be construed as a waiver or relinquishment to any extent of such Party's right to assert or rely upon any such provision, right, or remedy in that or any other instance; rather the same shall be and remain in full force and effect.
ollama_generated: 11.8 Non-Concession: For a waiver of any rights or obligations under this Agreement by a Party to be valid, it must be made in written form, signed by the Party responsible for the concession. Any leniency or delay on the part of either Party in demanding compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, or the omission to exercise any rights or remedies available under this Agreement or at law, shall not be construed as a concession or surrender of any extent of such Party's right to enforce or rely upon any provision, right, or remedy in this or any subsequent instance. Instead, such provisions shall continue to remain fully applicable and enforceable.

ACSI (Contractor) shall be obligated to ensure that the services rendered by them under this agreement meet the standards set forth in the Quality Assurance Plan provided by Company, failure to do so may result in a termination of the contract or withholding of payment for any substandard work. However, in the event that the Quality Assurance Plan is not provided, ACSI's services shall be deemed satisfactory unless Company can demonstrate, through objective evidence, that the services fail to meet reasonable industry standards.
